steps.title: Read the graph values
steps.content: From the graph, the estimated population is $6$ thousand at $x=0$ and $9$ thousand at $x=1$.

steps.title: Compare the populations
steps.content:
Divide the later population by the earlier population.
$$\frac{9}{6}=1.5$$
So the estimated population at $x=1$ is $1.5$ times the estimated population at $x=0$. The answer is B.
